questions.text: I believe that following through on commitments, even small ones, is a measure of character.
I believe that my sense of inner calm is one of the most stable things about me.
I tend to spend money based on what feels right in the moment rather than following a budget.
I believe that the clearest path through any emotionally complex situation is to focus on what can be known and done rather than on what is felt.
I believe that rapid experimentation teaches you more than patient, linear study.
When something excites me, the rush of energy is almost overwhelming.
I feel uneasy spending on luxuries for myself when others around me are struggling.
I tend to select projects based on how they will develop my personal expertise.
I feel that no quantity of reasoning can legitimately override the moral demand that arises from directly knowing of another's pain.
I usually do not notice when someone adjusts their behavior after getting a social signal from someone else.
I often yield to the group's preferred approach, even if mine differs.
I tend to propose ideas and invite feedback before moving forward.
I notice the precise moment when a group conversation becomes dominated by one person.
I find that I do my best thinking when I am focused on my own challenges.
I find that loud noises, surprises, or sudden changes don't produce a big reaction in me.
I have a bias toward action over deliberation.
I find that analytical clarity enables me to untangle complex situations where suffering has multiple causes and no simple solution.
I do not naturally notice when someone is managing their image in a social situation.
